vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1613: Substrate: agents stay live / are re-spawned on CLOSED issues (zombie agent slots)

plan.title: Substrate: agents stay live / are re-spawned on CLOSED issues (zombie agent slots)
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: The deacon resumes and re-dispatches work/review/test/ship/inspect agents on issues that are already in a terminal CLOSED/COMPLETED/CANCELLED state. No issue-state gate runs on the resume or dispatch paths, so zombie agents hold slots against maxAgents, burn tokens/compute (PAN-1496 ship agent at $9 climbing context on an issue closed 6 days prior), and drive OOM pressure. Tick-39 evidence shows the pipeline advanced a CLOSED issue review->ship. A robust isIssueClosed(issueId) helper already exists but is module-private and is consulted on only one narrow path (work-agent resume, trackerStatus==='closed' only).
narratives.Proposal: Extract the existing isIssueClosed/isTrackerIssueClosed helper (and its TTL cache) from orphan-proposed-reconciler.ts into a shared cloister module, then call it as a uniform gate on EVERY dispatch and resume entry point: the reactive chokepoint (service.ts onIssueStateChangePromise, which covers review/test/ship + the undispatched-ship patrol), the deacon review/test safety-net patrols, the inspect dispatch, the work-agent auto-resume path (broaden the existing narrow check), and the idle-work-agent nudge. Add a terminal-close teardown reaper in runPatrol() that actively stops any still-running agent whose parent issue is closed (mirroring postMergeLifecycle's teardown but for the terminal state), satisfying the issue's Expected #1. Invalidate the tracker-closed cache on `pan reopen` so a reopened issue is not wrongly gated by a stale 5-minute cache entry. The fix is scoped strictly to the cloister lifecycle layer; it does NOT modify the flywheel orchestrator (per the issue's explicit instruction).

autoDecisions.summary: Reuse and extract the existing isIssueClosed/isTrackerIssueClosed helper from orphan-proposed-reconciler.ts into a new shared module src/lib/cloister/issue-closed.ts rather than writing a new closed-check.
autoDecisions.rationale: The existing helper is already the most robust closed-detection in the codebase: it checks shadow-state (trackerStatus, shadowStatus, targetCanonicalState done/canceled) AND falls back to a live `gh issue view --json state` query with a 5-minute TTL cache. It already covers CLOSED/COMPLETED/CANCELLED uniformly. Duplicating logic would risk divergent definitions of 'closed'. Extraction keeps a single source of truth.

autoDecisions.summary: Gate at the dispatch/resume entry points, NOT inside spawnRun().
autoDecisions.rationale: spawnRun is also used legitimately by `pan reopen` and other manual paths, and gating it directly would couple the low-level primitive to tracker access and risk blocking legitimate reopen-driven spawns. The reactive chokepoint (service.ts:281) plus the named deacon patrols and inspect path cover every autonomous dispatch with fewer, clearer insertion points.

autoDecisions.summary: Include an active terminal-close teardown reaper (Expected #1), not just resume/dispatch gates.
autoDecisions.rationale: The gates prevent NEW spawns/resumes but an already-running zombie (e.g. the PAN-1496 ship agent climbing context at $9) keeps burning cost until it wedges. The issue's Expected list item #1 explicitly requires stopping running agents bound to a closed issue. A reaper in runPatrol() closes that gap. It uses stopAgent() (preserves workspace/branches/state — non-destructive) and only fires when the tracker issue is genuinely CLOSED, so it never touches verifying-on-main issues (whose tracker issue is still open until close-out).

autoDecisions.summary: Fold each gate's regression test into that gate's bead as an acceptance criterion rather than a single separate test bead.
autoDecisions.rationale: Each gate is independently testable in isolation (closed issue -> dispatch/resume function returns early without spawning). Co-locating the test with its gate keeps every bead independently reviewable and mergeable, matching the existing test layout (pan-871-auto-resume.test.ts, recover-orphaned-agents-grace.test.ts).

items.title: Extract shared isIssueClosed helper into src/lib/cloister/issue-closed.ts

narrative.Action: Move isIssueClosed(issueId, closedIssueIds?) and isTrackerIssueClosed(issueId) plus the trackerClosedCache / TRACKER_CLOSED_CACHE_TTL_MS into a new shared module src/lib/cloister/issue-closed.ts and export them. Also export a clearIssueClosedCache(issueId?: string) function that deletes a single issue's cache entry (or clears all when no arg). Re-point orphan-proposed-reconciler.ts to import isIssueClosed and the cache-clear from the new module with NO behavior change there (the existing trackerClosedCache.clear() at line 381 should call the new exported clear). This is the foundation contract every downstream gate imports.

subItems.title: New module src/lib/cloister/issue-closed.ts exports isIssueClosed, isTrackerIssueClosed, and clearIssueClosedCache, preserving the existing semantics (shadow trackerStatus/shadowStatus==='closed', targetCanonicalState 'done'|'canceled', live gh fallback, 5-min TTL cache).

orphan-proposed-reconciler.ts imports isIssueClosed (and clearIssueClosedCache for its existing reset) from the new module; its own copies are deleted; its existing tests still pass unchanged (no behavior change).

A unit test for the new module asserts isIssueClosed returns true for each of trackerStatus==='closed', shadowStatus==='closed', targetCanonicalState==='done', targetCanonicalState==='canceled', and via the gh tracker fallback; and false for an open issue. Tracker-poll behavior is mocked (no real gh call).
npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and npm test pass.

items.title: Gate the reactive dispatch chokepoint (service.ts onIssueStateChangePromise) on isIssueClosed

narrative.Action: In src/lib/cloister/service.ts onIssueStateChangePromise, after the `const role = stateToRole(newState)` null-check (around service.ts:281, before the activeRoleRunExists probe), add an early return when isIssueClosed(normalizedIssueId) is true: log a clear '[cloister] <issue>: skipping <role> dispatch — issue is closed' line, emit an info activity entry, and return without spawning. This single guard covers reactive review/test/ship dispatch AND the undispatched-ship safety-net patrol (which routes through onIssueStateChange('shipping')).

subItems.title: onIssueStateChangePromise returns early (no spawnReviewRoleForIssue, no dispatchTestAgentAndNotify, no spawnRun) when isIssueClosed(issueId) is true, for review, test, and ship roles.
When the issue is NOT closed, dispatch behavior is unchanged (existing reactive-dispatch tests still pass).
A test asserts that onIssueStateChange(issueId,'shipping') on a closed issue does NOT dispatch ship, and the same for 'in_review' and 'testing'. isIssueClosed is stubbed to true.
npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and npm test pass.

items.title: Gate the deacon review/test safety-net patrols on isIssueClosed

narrative.Action: The deacon patrols re-dispatch review/test agents directly, bypassing the reactive chokepoint. Add an isIssueClosed gate so each one skips closed issues: checkOrphanedReviewStatuses per-issue loop (add the gate at the top of the loop alongside the existing status.stuck / status.deaconIgnored continues near deacon.ts:1449-1452 — this single insertion covers BOTH the orphaned-review re-dispatch at ~1615 and the orphaned-test re-dispatch at ~1683); the stuck-review re-dispatch (~deacon.ts:1784); the additional review re-dispatch patrol (~deacon.ts:2331); and the pending/retry-test dispatch (~deacon.ts:1862). Each skip should log '[deacon] <issue>: skipping <role> re-dispatch — issue is closed'. checkUndispatchedShip routes through onIssueStateChange and is already covered by gate-reactive-dispatch — do NOT duplicate a gate there.

subItems.title: checkOrphanedReviewStatuses skips closed issues for both its review and test re-dispatch branches (verified by reading the diff at the loop guard).
The stuck-review re-dispatch (~1784), the second review re-dispatch patrol (~2331), and the pending/retry-test dispatch (~1862) each skip when isIssueClosed is true.
A test asserts each patrol does NOT call spawnReviewRoleForIssue / spawnRun('test') when isIssueClosed is stubbed true, and DOES dispatch normally when false.
No gate is added to checkUndispatchedShip (it is covered by the reactive chokepoint gate) — confirmed by inspection.
npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and npm test pass.

narrative.Action: In src/lib/cloister/inspect-agent.ts spawnInspectAgentPromise (~inspect-agent.ts:142, before the session-exists check), add an isIssueClosed guard that logs '[cloister] <issue>: skipping inspect dispatch — issue is closed' and returns without building the tmux launcher. The inspect path does not use spawnRun, so it needs its own gate.

subItems.title: spawnInspectAgentPromise returns early without spawning an inspect session when isIssueClosed(issueId) is true.
A test asserts no inspect tmux launcher is created for a closed issue, and normal inspect dispatch occurs when the issue is open.
npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and npm test pass.

items.title: Broaden the work-agent auto-resume closed-gate to use shared isIssueClosed

narrative.Action: In src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts autoResumeStoppedWorkAgents (the existing narrow gate at ~deacon.ts:5472-5477 that only checks shadowState?.trackerStatus === 'closed'), replace the inline check with a call to the shared isIssueClosed(state.issueId). This broadens the resume skip to also cover COMPLETED/CANCELLED (targetCanonicalState 'done'|'canceled', shadowStatus==='closed') and the live tracker fallback, closing the gap where a closed-but-not-trackerStatus-closed issue currently slips through and gets its work agent auto-resumed.

subItems.title: autoResumeStoppedWorkAgents skips auto-resume (continue) when isIssueClosed(issueId) is true, including the COMPLETED/CANCELLED cases that the old trackerStatus-only check missed.
A test asserts a stopped work agent on an issue with targetCanonicalState==='done' (but trackerStatus not 'closed') is NOT auto-resumed; an open issue's stopped work agent still resumes.
Existing auto-resume tests (e.g. pan-871-auto-resume.test.ts) still pass.
npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and npm test pass.

narrative.Action: In src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts nudgeIdleWorkAgentsWithOpenBeads (~deacon.ts:5255), add an isIssueClosed guard so the patrol does NOT nudge a live work/inspect agent whose parent issue is closed. Nudging a zombie keeps it alive and burns tokens; skip closed issues. Place the gate after the role==='work' filter so only closed-issue work agents are skipped.

subItems.title: nudgeIdleWorkAgentsWithOpenBeads does not send a nudge to a work agent when isIssueClosed(issueId) is true; open-issue idle agents are still nudged.
A test asserts no nudge message is delivered for a closed issue's idle work agent.
npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and npm test pass.

items.title: Add a terminal-close teardown reaper that stops running agents on closed issues

narrative.Action: Add a new reconciler (e.g. src/lib/cloister/closed-issue-reaper.ts, exporting reconcileClosedIssueAgents()) and wire it into runPatrol() (deacon.ts ~4212, alongside the other reconcilers like reconcileOrphanProposedSpecs). It enumerates running agents via listRunningAgents(), and for each agent whose parent state.issueId resolves isIssueClosed===true, stops it via stopAgent(agentId) (which kills the tmux session, kills the orphan launcher, and marks state stopped — workspace, branches, and state.json are preserved; this is NOT deep-wipe). It must be idempotent (skip agents already stopped) and emit a clear activity log per reaped agent ('[deacon] reaped <agentId> — parent issue <issue> is closed'). To bound cost, dedupe isIssueClosed calls per issueId within a single patrol pass (the helper's 5-min cache already amortizes the live gh fallback across passes).

subItems.title: reconcileClosedIssueAgents() stops every running agent (any role: work, review, test, ship, inspect) whose parent issue isIssueClosed===true, using stopAgent() — never deep-wipe and never teardownWorkspace.
It does NOT stop agents on open issues, on verifying_on_main issues (whose tracker issue is still open), or agents already in stopped/error state (idempotent — a second pass is a no-op).
The reaper is invoked from runPatrol() and is guarded by the same no-resume / globally-paused gates the sibling reconcilers respect (it does not run when the deacon is globally paused).

A test asserts: a running ship agent on a closed issue is stopped; a running work agent on an open issue is left alone; a stopped agent on a closed issue is not touched; a second invocation reaps nothing new.
isIssueClosed is evaluated at most once per distinct issueId per patrol pass.
npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and npm test pass.

items.title: Invalidate the tracker-closed cache when an issue is reopened

narrative.Action: In the `pan reopen` path (src/cli/commands/reopen.ts and/or the reopen workflow in src/lib/lifecycle/workflows.ts), call clearIssueClosedCache(issueId) after the issue is reopened so the new gates do not wrongly treat the just-reopened issue as closed for up to 5 minutes due to a stale TTL cache entry. This prevents a 'reopened but nothing spawns' confusion window.

subItems.title: The reopen path calls clearIssueClosedCache(issueId) so a subsequent isIssueClosed(issueId) re-queries fresh state instead of returning a cached 'closed'.
A test asserts the cache entry for the issue is cleared during reopen (or that isIssueClosed returns false immediately after reopen given fresh tracker state).
npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and npm test pass.

review.notes: Inspect-session reaping routes inspect-* through stopAgent normalization, so closed inspect tmux sessions stay live

history.notes: Inspect-session reaping routes inspect-* through stopAgent normalization, so closed inspect tmux sessions stay live
